messages.subject: [OGRUG] R-UG-Ottawa Digest, Vol 34, Issue 1
messages.body_plain:
Hi
I'm a new member of the Ottawa R User Group.

I started  a course on R at EdX <https://www.edx.org/>. It's a free 
5-week online course that started on September 9. It's not too late to 
register because the deadline to turn in the first week's graded 
questions is September 22. Check it out here: Explore Statistics With R 
<https://www.edx.org/course/kix/kix-kiexplorx-explore-statistics-r-1524#.VBGaz5K3Kx4> 
.

I think R-UG-Ottawa should create a group in Meetup 
<http://www.meetup.com/>. That's the first place I looked for other R 
users in Ottawa.

Thanks,
Pierre Tourigny

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [OGRUG] R meetups and OCRUG
messages.body_plain:
On 09/11/2014 08:54 AM, Pierre Tourigny wrote:
> Hi
> I'm a new member of the Ottawa R User Group.
>
> I started  a course on R at EdX <https://www.edx.org/>. It's a free
> 5-week online course that started on September 9. It's not too late to
> register because the deadline to turn in the first week's graded
> questions is September 22. Check it out here: Explore Statistics With R
> <https://www.edx.org/course/kix/kix-kiexplorx-explore-statistics-r-1524#.VBGaz5K3Kx4>
> .
>
> I think R-UG-Ottawa should create a group in Meetup
> <http://www.meetup.com/>. That's the first place I looked for other R
> users in Ottawa.

There already is a Meetup group, although I think there haven't been any 
meetings in awhile. It is always a bit difficult to decide whether the 
OCRUG style meetings with a presentation work better, or the more casual 
meetups. Probably a mix is useful. But if there are too many meetings 
then none are well attended.

Paul

>
> Thanks,
> Pierre Tourigny
>
> 	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
>
> _______________________________________________
> R-UG-Ottawa mailing list
> R-UG-Ottawa at r-project.org
> https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-ug-ottawa
>
